Because God has forgiven you for what Jesus did, you should follow God and imitate him. Show his love and kindness, as those who are loved by your heavenly Father. Christ’s sacrifice demonstrates his great love, and we must fully appreciate it.

You must get rid of immoral desires. These sins must be feared and hated. Here are not only warnings against obvious sinful acts but also against things that some may dismiss as unimportant. But these things are so harmful that they pollute and damage those who hear them. Your happiness should show itself as Christians, in ways that honor God. A greedy person makes money their god; they place hope, confidence, and joy in worldly possessions, which should be for God alone. Those who indulge in immoral desires or love the world do not belong to God’s kingdom of grace, nor will they enter his kingdom of glory. When even the worst sinners repent and believe the good news, they become obedient children, from whom God's anger is turned away. Should we dismiss what brings down God’s wrath? Sinners, like people lost in darkness, are going they do not know where and doing they do not know what. But God’s grace brought a powerful change to many souls. Live as children of light, with knowledge and holiness. These dark deeds are unproductive, no matter how much profit they may claim; for they end in the destruction of those who do not repent. There are many ways to support or participate in other people's sins: by praising them, advising them, agreeing with them, or hiding them. And if you share in others’ sins, expect to suffer their consequences. If you fail to correct other people's sins, you are participating with them. A good person will be ashamed to talk about what many wicked people do without shame. We must not only recognize that sin is wrong and shameful but see it as a violation of God’s holy law. Following the example of prophets and apostles, we should call on those who are spiritually asleep and dead to wake up so that Christ may give them light.

Another way to avoid sin is to be careful; otherwise, you cannot maintain purity of heart and life. Time is a gift from God, and it is wasted when not used as he intends. If you have wasted your time in the past, you must work even harder now. How little do people think about the time that thousands on their deathbeds would gladly trade for the whole world? How quickly they sacrifice it to unimportant things! People often complain about difficult times; if they did, they would be more eager to use their time wisely. Do not be foolish. Ignoring your duty and neglecting your soul shows great folly. Drunkenness is a sin that leads to other evils; it provokes God greatly. A drunkard presents a sad example to his family and the world—a person who has become hardened beyond repair, rushing toward destruction. When you are afflicted or weary, do not try to feel better with strong drink, which is hateful, harmful, and only makes your sorrows worse. Instead, seek to be filled with the Spirit through prayer and avoid anything that might upset our gracious Comforter. All of God’s people have reason to rejoice. Even when we are not singing, we should always give thanks; we should never lack a desire for this duty, just as we never lack something to be thankful for throughout our lives. Always, even in trials and difficulties, and for everything; being confident that their intentions are loving and beneficial. God keeps believers from sinning against him and encourages them to treat each other with kindness in all he has commanded, to promote his glory, and to fulfill their duties to one another.

The duty of wives is to submit to their husbands according to the Lord’s will, which includes honoring and obeying them out of love. The duty of husbands is to love their wives. Christ's love for the church is an example—a love that is sincere, pure, and constant, even when she fails. Christ gave himself for the church so he could make it holy in this world and glorify it in the next, so he could give all his followers a principle of holiness and deliver them from guilt, pollution, and the power of sin through the influence of the Holy Spirit, which baptism symbolizes. The church and believers will not be without fault or imperfection until they reach glory. But only those who are made holy now will be glorified later. The words Adam spoke, mentioned by the apostle, refer literally to marriage; but they also have a deeper meaning relating to the union between Christ and his church. It was a type of this relationship. There will be failures and shortcomings on both sides in human relationships, yet that does not change the nature of the relationship. All the duties of marriage are included in unity and love. And while we admire and rejoice in God’s loving kindness, let husbands and wives learn from it their duties to each other. This would prevent many evils and avoid much pain.
